_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use commas to mark prepositional phrases in sentences, and how apostrophes work when more than one person owns something.

_source.w: We are learning to use commas to mark prepositional phrases in sentences, and how apostrophes work when more than one person owns something.
y: Correct punctuation makes our writing clearer and more professional.
Understanding commas with phrases and apostrophes with plural possession addresses two common writing challenges.
These skills demonstrate growing mastery of English writing conventions.
eg: We use commas to set off prepositional phrases at the start of sentences — 'After the storm, we went outside.'
We learn the difference between 'the student's book' (one student) and 'the students' books' (many students).
We practise placing apostrophes correctly for both singular and plural possession.
We identify prepositional phrases in our reading and notice how commas are used with them.
We edit a text that has missing commas and incorrect apostrophes.
We create examples that demonstrate the difference between singular and plural possession.
